title: strange error on restart "docker-entrypoint.sh": executable file not found in $PATH
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
when I restart a container I get this error message:
```
Error: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: exec: "docker-entrypoint.sh": executable file not found in $PATH: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```
but the container is running correctly

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Wed Nov 10 02:48:06 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@christophemacabiau Are you still having this issue.  Have you updated to the latest code?
can you please provide a way to reproduce it?

So far nothing clear, not easy to reproduce
One point: I have this issue on a Rocky Linux release 8.5 server; the same image restart normally on Ubuntu 21.04, @giuseppe does this remind you of anything?

> One point: I have this issue on a Rocky Linux release 8.5 server; the same image restart normally on Ubuntu 21.04, @giuseppe does this remind you of anything?

I don't remember anything related to RHEL 8.5.  It would also be helpful to know what image are you using and how the container was created and restarted, at least I can try reproducing it here.

ok I think I have something similar with this pod:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

podman pod exists podmantest && podman pod rm -f podmantest
podman pod create --name podmantest

podman run -d --pod podmantest --name postgrespodman \
  -e POSTGRES_USER=postgres -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres \
  --security-opt label=disable \
  docker.io/postgres:latest

podman exec postgrespodman "psql --help"
```
I get this error:
```
Error: exec failed: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: exec: "psql --help": executable file not found in $PATH: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```

Sorry for not being more helpful, I have not enough knowledge of podman. Maybe it comes from the server configuration?
Ask me any debug information you need, I will try to help you.

`"psql --help"` is passed down as a single argument, so it is looking for a binary called `"psql --help"`.  I think you want to specify these options separately:

`podman exec postgrespodman psql --help`
ok you are right I will try to reproduce the initial bug starting from my pod
Does that solve this issue, IE Can we close it?

sorry i am unable to reproduce it on a simple example, the error message does not help me :-)
if I can reproduce it later I will reopen a bug
